diagnostic_explanation: Both reviewers assigned the same unsupported label and used no citations, so there is no citation difference. The difference is in how they coded the lack of support: option_1 uses the more specific code central_proposition_unsupported, while option_2 uses the broader code insufficient_evidence. Given the claim trust_single_metric and the evidence showing only that a metric improved, users reported harm, the metric was incomplete, and a harm check was later added, both reason codes are defensible and compatible rather than conflicting. Their rationales are substantively aligned, with both noting that no evidence supports trusting a single metric and that evidence about incompleteness cuts against such trust. Confidence is identical at high.
